opp.ais_why:
• Zero Avoma meetings on file; SF Description null. Product scope has Merlin and Merlin Intake plus a broad S2P suite (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iRequest, iRisk, iSource, iSupplier). No customer dialogue evidence to confirm AI is driving the conversation. The two AI SKUs (Merlin + Merlin Intake) in scope provide a faint AI signal but it's not corroborated by any captured engagement.
• Qualified stage at $740K (the largest amount in chunk 4), BD_Engagement Unengaged, LastActivity 2026-03-16 ‚Äî about 2 months of dormancy. Despite the strategic deal size, the lack of meeting activity indicates early or cold engagement. Galp Energia is a Portuguese integrated energy company; the deal could be a strategic foothold if engagement activates.
• Low confidence: thin metadata, no Avoma, no description. AI in scope is the only signal. Defaulted DOWN at 4/5 due to absent engagement evidence. Would move UP only with a recorded engagement that surfaces Galp buyer interest in Merlin/Merlin Intake; would drop with continued dormancy beyond 6 months.

items.quote: Asked Hemendra to enlarge the knowledge of this account, have more Stakeholders
Already asked to help them to work on a B Case
no Avoma calls found for this opp

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or ROI is documented. Next Step entries from July through September 2025 show the rep repeatedly offering to help Galp build a business case to maximise their chance of internally validating the project, which itself confirms no approved metrics or budget existed then. The stated need per Next Step 2026-06-16 is a more performant S2P solution, but no cost, cycle-time, headcount, or error-rate impact has ever been captured. Quantifying the value with Alfredo and a finance stakeholder is the move, but it requires the project to restart first.

champion.narrative: Alfredo Figueira (Head of Commercial and Corporate Procurement Services) is the single identified sponsor, and his champion strength is weak and at risk. Every Next Step entry from May 2025 through June 2026 names him as the sole contact. He has shown some advocacy, joining the Showpad workspace in June 2025, asking to add Tania Madeira, and staying in touch through the hold (confirming March 2026 he would keep Zycus informed). But he has no documented access to the Decision Maker Carlos Silva, has not introduced Zycus to IT or transformation stakeholders despite the rep's November 2025 requests to widen the base, and has been passive since the hold began. If he is deprioritised post-merger, Zycus has zero fallback coverage.

sources: SF Competitors__c: iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba
competition.narrative: Three competitors are logged in Salesforce (iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba), but there is no active competitive evaluation because the deal stalled before any RFP or shortlist. No Next Step, task, or call references a head-to-head, a preferred rival, or a bake-off, so the current competitive threat is dormant rather than live. If the project restarts post-merger it will re-enter as a fresh full-suite RFP against all three. The wedge is to lead with Merlin and Merlin Intake (agentic intake, sourcing, and risk) as an AI-maturity differentiator rather than compete on feature parity with the incumbents.

identify_pain.narrative: Operational pain is acknowledged but not quantified or multi-threaded. Next Step 2026-06-16 states Galp still needs a more performant S2P solution, so the pain (likely current-system performance, usability, or integration gaps) remains real despite the hold. However no pain owner beyond Alfredo is named, no quantified impact is captured, and the pain has never been validated with IT (Marta Faustino), transformation (Rui Manuel Riberio Pereira, Bruno Meireles), or the Decision Maker (Carlos Silva). It sits single-threaded and unconfirmed at the executive level. A multi-threaded discovery on restart is needed to re-establish and size the pain.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, procurement, or InfoSec mechanics are visible. The deal has not moved beyond Qualified, so MSA, SOW, redlining, and security review are entirely undocumented. EY Spain appears as a partner in Next Step entries from June 2025, but whether they act as an implementation SI, co-sell partner, or procurement advisor is unclear and no partner agreement is recorded. No current contract expiration is present to act as a forcing function. Visibility will only emerge if the project restarts and advances toward Negotiation.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is identified or engaged. Contact roles name Carlos Silva as Decision Maker (Head of Global Procurement and Contracts), but no activity, task, or Next Step entry shows any contact with Carlos or any C-level executive across the entire lifecycle. Every touch routes through Alfredo Figueira (Head of Commercial and Corporate Procurement). Next Step entries from September through December 2025 repeatedly state the project is stopped awaiting a management decision, yet no executive access was ever secured. Without budget authority the deal cannot restart post-merger. The move is to open a path to Carlos Silva or a CFO-level sponsor to test whether procurement transformation survives the Moev integration.

decision_process.narrative: The buying and approval process is unknown. Next Step entries from September through December 2025 repeatedly reference an internal management decision on whether to restart, but no named approval chain, procurement path, or governance step is recorded. The Galp-Moev partnership signed March 2026 (Next Step 2026-06-16) has become the overriding gate: priorities are frozen pending that integration. EY Spain is referenced as a partner in June 2025 entries but its role in any decision process is undefined. Mapping who decides post-merger, and by when, is the core missing piece.

decision_criteria.narrative: No evaluation criteria are documented. The deal never reached an RFI, RFP, ShoeFit, or use-case validation stage, and there are no Avoma calls to surface stated requirements. The only directional signal is the broad S2P product scope (iSource, iContract, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRequest, iRisk plus Merlin and Merlin Intake) implying a full-suite replacement rather than a point solution. The RFP that Alfredo promised for March 2026 (Next Step 2025-12-11) would have carried the criteria but was never issued. Capturing the buyer's weighted requirements is a restart-gated move.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 10 is carried by momentum 55 vs stage-expected 50; competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15). Offsetting it: preference strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 55 (moving forward): Next Step carries 8 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (10), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 55, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 8 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c 4.0, AIS_Status__c Moderate: the two AI SKUs in scope provide a faint signal but no captured engagement confirms Galp is pulling on AI. No calls exist to show it under or over-rated.

business_case.evidence: No ROI or savings quantified; rep repeatedly offered to help build a business case through 2025, indicating none exists.

Rep set a close-lost trigger if no feedback by 2026-07-01
Alfredo replied a restart is unlikely before end 2028

deal_movement.summary: The pulse is dark. The only 2026 movement is rep-side: close date pushed to March 2027 on 2026-03-16 when the project was suspended, and two follow-up emails to Alfredo in June 2026 that confirmed the hold rather than restarting it. No buyer-driven progression in over two years in Qualified.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is dark and cooling further. The last buyer message (2026-06-16) pushed a restart toward 2028, and the rep's self-set July 1 2026 close-lost trigger has passed with no positive signal.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2024-07-01 (about 733 days old) and in Qualified for the entire lifecycle with no stage progression. Close date 2027-03-16 sits about 256 days out but has already been pushed three times (2025-09-15, 2025-10-14, 2026-03-16). The deal has been on strategic hold for about 109 days since 2026-03-16. Last touch 2026-06-16 was a rep-side email; Alfredo replied a restart is unlikely before end 2028. The Next Step self-set a close-lost trigger for no feedback by 2026-07-01, now passed. Pace required to close by March 2027 is unachievable without an immediate restart, executive sponsorship, and a full enterprise cycle.
evidence: Next Step 2026-06-16: on hold following Galp-Moev partnership signed March 2026, no visibility on priorities, next update mid-September 2026
Task 2026-06-16: Alfredo Figueira email says a restart, if any, is believed to be before end 2028
Next Step 2026-06-01: if no feedback by 2026-07-01, close as lost due to the merger
Next Step 2026-03-16: Alfredo confirmed the project is suspended due to the potential Moev merger
Next Step 2025-12-11: Alfredo said an RFP would be sent March 2026 (never occurred)
Field history: CloseDate pushed 2025-09-15, 2025-10-14, and 2026-03-16 to 2027-03-16
OpportunityContactRole: Carlos Silva is Decision Maker but has no engagement record in any activity or Next Step entry
No Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor contact role present; all touches route through Alfredo Figueira
north_star_verdict.headline: Project suspended since March 2026 pending the Galp-Moev merger; single-threaded to one sponsor, no executive access, and the rep's own July 1 2026 close-lost trigger has now passed.

flags: Break the single-thread: reach Carlos Silva (Decision Maker) directly; Alfredo alone cannot restart a merger-frozen project.
No Economic Buyer or budget owner is mapped; without executive sponsorship the deal cannot survive the Moev reprioritisation.
Make a keep-or-kill call: the rep's own July 1 2026 close-lost trigger has passed with a restart pushed toward 2028.
best_practice_check.summary: This deal is frozen by the Galp-Moev merger and single-threaded to one passive sponsor. Winning it later depends on breaking the single-thread, securing executive sponsorship, and making a decision now on whether to keep it open or disqualify.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer call evidence; only rep-side outreach on record. Alfredo courteous but signals no restart before end 2028.

competitive_position.summary: No live competitive fight: the deal stalled before any RFP or shortlist, so all three logged rivals are dormant, not active. iValua, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba remain on record from Competitors__c but none has been named in any call, task, or Next Step as preferred or evaluated. The real threat to this deal is not a competitor, it is the Galp-Moev merger freezing procurement priorities. If a fresh RFP issues post-merger, expect all three back in a full-suite bake-off.
